item.title: Re: Fermot's Spiral and...
item.content:encoded:
Ah-ha. Thanks for that. I underappreciated the simplicity in nature of this SCP's containment protocol. The Foundation doesn't always have to instate a large-scale, or an amnestic driven cover-up.

Thanks for the reply Dankaar.

item.title: Re: Fermot's Spiral and...
item.content:encoded:
Because it apparently doesn't happen often, and saying that the memory of the SCP is the result of mental trama from a (presumably) stillbirth or baby born with horrific birth defects does work (when pared with hypnotherapy).

And I'm pretty sure most people would rather beleive that the mishapen inside-out monstrosity that came out of someone is a result of some kind of mental trauma due to circumstances around the event, rather than a memory of what actually happened.

item.title: Fermot's Spiral and...
item.content:encoded:
I think I get it. The fact that it operates on the spiral, and produces two simultaneous occurrences that are basically a genealogical swap of the parents, makes the theorist in me shudder. This is gross and off-putting in so many ways and that earns it my +1.

Also, adding to the fact that all known amnesiacs don't work with this thing and all attempted predictive algorithms were exhausted (even while technically following one), why isn't this Keter? All it takes is a seemingly remote occurrence for this to suddenly start a widespread information breach. I, frankly, recommend this be reclassified Keter unless someone can point-out something I'm missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really made me squirm and it was evocative enough to get the +1. I don't love the addendum being like "oh this is really ramping up look out", but it's not enough of a detraction, just doesn't feel necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that you can't forget the weird looking thing just isn't working for me. Feels shoehorned at the last minute.

item.title: Re: FUUUUU
item.content:encoded: <pedant>He didn't disappear, he strangled himself with the umbilical cord.</pedant>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet Pelor, this was disturbing. The birthdate is within spitting distance of my own, too.

item.title: Re: FUUUUU
item.content:encoded: Yes. The Butterfly Effect, specifically the director's cut.

item.title: Re: FUUUUU
item.content:encoded: Believe it's The Butterfly Effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reworked the line to make it less creepy pasta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anything that makes me sit here this long processing what I just read gets an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
will be automatically alerted to investigate and, if necessary, recover instances of SCP-2910-1.

Do you mean 2910-A? A 2910-1 isn't mentioned anywhere else in the article other than the document in the procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easily +1able, despite - yet again - a complete absence of rabid glowing monkeys in a mainlist scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
High on the body-horror scale, but this line

including its cries and crawling gait.

feels more like bad creepypasta.

item.title: Re: FUUUUU
item.content:encoded: That was not Looper.

item.title: FUUUUU
item.content:encoded:
What's the name of that movie where the guy can travel through time and disappears from his mom's womb into the past, making her think she miscarried? There's more to it, but I can't remember…
Was it looper?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty gross but I liked it! 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of an actual phenomenon where the mother's fetus just straight up disappears.

What I like about this is that it does leave room for interpretation and, moreover, questioning! Why could this be happening more frequently I wonder? Or, more appropriately, why is it happening at all? It truly is an anomaly- one that seems to be difficult if not impossible to stop in reality and in memory. Regardless, I like the way it's written. +1

Also it's nice to know a fun thread of mine got someone to come up with their own cool concept :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is a very creepy skip with a very old school feel to it. Its got some nice body horror going for it, and is short, sweet, and to the point.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. There are some implications that don't really go anywhere and don't add to the scp but this is a crawling ball of baby organs you can literally never forget and that's creative and horrifying in the best way

item.title: Baby Not on Board
item.content:encoded:
I haven't written an actual SCP in half a year. This is me trying to get back in the saddle with some actual spooky nonsense. Inspired by a Two-Sentence Horror post I made, my own sordid attempts at writing SCPs, and the gruesome tale of one Mrs. Mary Toft.

Thanks to DrGrym, hornetcorset, Jacob Conwell, Jenfwoosh, minmin, TheDuckman, TwistedGears,shaggydredlocks, Some_Potato, and Wils for feedback.

See more of my work on my author page!
